ahkimn/subwaybuilder-regions.name: Regions v0.4.9
ahkimn/subwaybuilder-regions.body:
## v0.4.9 - 2026-04-26

_Game version_ v1.3.0

### New Features

- Added CZ region support for:
  - Okresy (`okres` | districts)
  - Obce (`obce` | municipalities)
  - ZSJ-díl (`zsj` | basic settlement units)
- Released initial datasets for all seven currently released cities for Czechia with boundary data sourced from RÚIAN / CZSO.

### Other Updates

- Added local static recognition for downloaded CZ city datasets so release/distributed CZ data can be discovered by the mod even though CZ is not supported by the runtime fetch wrappers (same as JP).
- Removed CA city codes from the static custom city/country mapping; CA datasets are now resolved solely through the online served index.

### Bugfixes

- Fixed `Overview Panel` crash caused by a React hook-order violation when switching to the WIP `Historical Data` or `Ridership` tabs; both tabs are now hidden and any stale saved tab state resets to `Overview` on mount.

- Fixed modded cities incorrectly being excluded from filtering on download

ahkimn/subwaybuilder-regions.name: v0.4.6
ahkimn/subwaybuilder-regions.body:
## v0.4.6 - 2026-04-02

_Game version_ v1.2.0

### New Features

- Added JP region support for:
  - Municipalities (`shichouson` | 市町村)
  - Ooaza (`ooaza` | 大字)
- Added bilingual JP region labels in `Japanese\nEnglish` format with romaji generation for output names.
- Station O/D Ridership added to both the `Info Panel` and the `Overview Panel`

### Other Updates

- Added local static recognition for downloaded JP city datasets so release/distributed JP data can be discovered by the mod even though JP is not supported by the runtime fetch wrappers.

### Bugfixes

- Stopped unresolved folder open actions from preventing validation (esp. on Linux) by timing out the block after a set period of time
- Removed undownloaded city codes (as well as city codes from countries with no available datasets) from being shown on the Settings Menu

ahkimn/subwaybuilder-regions.name: v0.4.5
ahkimn/subwaybuilder-regions.body:
## v0.4.5 - 2026-03-04

_Game version_ v1.1.0

### New Features

- Added GB `wpcs` support for Westminster Parliamentary Constituencies (ONS July 2024 boundaries).
- Added online fetch support for CA `peds` via hosted `.geojson.gz` source (no longer local-only in runtime fetch flows).
- Added shared remote compressed GeoJSON loader utility for script pipelines so hosted binary `.geojson.gz` sources can be reused across datasets/countries.

### Other Updates

- GB population attachment is now sourced from NOMIS API for all GB datasets (`districts`, `wpcs`, `bua`, `wards`)
  - Deprecated local CSV population matching path in GB extraction scripts.

kimth9/sb_korean_transit_pack_v2.name: [KTP] Hot fix(v1.0.3)
kimth9/sb_korean_transit_pack_v2.body:
Korean Transit Pack

If you are looking for the perfect public transit options to accompany the "Korean Map Pack," this is the must-have download for you!

1. Included Transit Systems

  • GTX (Great Train eXpress) based on GTX-A | In-game Code : [KR] GTX
  • Korean-style Heavy Metro based on Seoul Line 1 | In-game Code : [KR] Heavy Metro
  • Korean-style Light Rail Transit based on Incheon Line 2 | In-game Code : [KR] Light Rail Transit
  • Korean-style Tram based on Wirye Line | In-game Code : [KR] Tram
  • Korean-style BRT based on Sejong BRT | In-game Code : [KR] BRT

All vehicle specifications, construction costs, and operating expenses have been meticulously balanced based on authoritative and official data.

2. Vehicle Info

GTX Heavy Metro Light Rail Transit Tram BRT
MAX speed 180km/h 110km/h 80km/h 60km/h 60km/h
Capacity per car 173 people 160 people 103 people 49 people 43 people
Real-world car set 8-car set 4, 6, 8, 10-car set Usually 2, 4, 6-car set 5-module set 2-module set
minimun / maximum cars 8-car only 4, 6, 8, 10 2, 4, 6 5, 10 2-car only
Car length 20m/car 19.5m/car 16.35m/car 7m/module 9.1m/module
Platform Lenght 165~205m 85m~210m 35m~100m 40m~80m 20m~40m
Construction Cost
(below -24m)		 1x 3x 3x 3x 24x
Construction Cost
(-24m~-10m)		 9x 1.4x 1.4x 1.4x 5.3x
Construction Cost
(-10m~-4m)		 9x 1x 1x 1x 2.6x
Construction Cost
(-4m~4.5m)		 9x 0.55x 0.55x 0.55x 1x
Construction Cost
(above 4.5m)		 9x 1.6x 1.6x 1.6x 3.3x

3. Data Sources

The data used in this pack is derived from the following sources:

  • GTX Data
    • GTX-A Preliminary Feasibility Study Report (Korea Development Institute [KDI], 2024)
    • Western Metropolitan Area Wide-Area Express Railway (GTX-D) Preliminary Feasibility Study Report (KDI, 2025)
  • Heavy Metro Data
    • Statistical Yearbook of Korean Railroads (Ministry of Land, Infrastructure and Transport, 2024)
    • Shinbundang Line Northwest Extension (Yongsan~Samsong) Preliminary Feasibility Study Report (KDI, 2023)
  • Light Rail Transit Data
    • Incheon Metro Line 2 Rolling Stock Data
    • Mokdong Line (Sinwol-dong~Dangsan) Preliminary Feasibility Study Report (KDI, 2025)
  • Tram Data
    • Seoul Wirye Line Tram (Upcoming) Rolling Stock Data
    • Ulsan Subway Line 1 Re-feasibility Study Report (KDI, 2023)
  • BRT Data
    • Hyundai Electric Articulated Bus Rolling Stock Data
    • Cheongna~Hwagok BRT Preliminary Feasibility Study Report (KDI, 2006)
  • Consumer Price Index (CPI) from the Bank of Korea Economic Statistics System (ECOS)

4. Future Update Roadmap

This map pack was developed using data from the following institutions:

  • This pack will be updated periodically to include the latest data and ensure compatibility with future game updates.
  • Monorail and Maglev may be include if good data will be searched

5. Feedback & Contact

If you have any suggestions, bug reports, or ideas for the maps, please feel free to share them with me on discord channel! Thank you for playing!

6. Changelog

Changed Train Operation Cost which was over-counted.

It's now 1/10 in GTX and Heavy Metro

danield1909/dantrains.name: Version 1.3.2 "Phoenix" (Hotfix)
danield1909/dantrains.body:
1.3.2 Patch Notes:
- Added horizontal scroll
- Fixed bug for 1.3.0

danield1909/dantrains.name: Version 1.3.1 "Phoenix" (Hotfix)
danield1909/dantrains.body:
- Added FAQ Panel
- Fixed multiple save related glitches
- Added scrolling to panels
- Fixed some glitches with settings menu
- Fixed wrong train capacity for DLR07 thanks to notrickastley on discord.

danield1909/dantrains.name: Version 1.3.0 "Phoenix" (Compatibility Update)
danield1909/dantrains.body:
- Added 1.2.2 beta compatibility
    - Track maintenance costs subject to change
- Fixed various glitches related to the save loader
- Added 6 trams from Melbourne

danield1909/dantrains.name: Version 1.2.1 "Phoenix" (Hotfix)
danield1909/dantrains.body:
- Fixed a glitch which caused newly registered trains to have dramatically incorrect maxSlopePercentages
    - To fix any trains affected, simply enter the game, save again, and then reload

danield1909/dantrains.name: Version 1.2.0 "Phoenix" (Minor Update)
danield1909/dantrains.body:
- Adjusted minCurveRadii to use a new formula.
    - If the new value smaller, the track will automatically update existing tracks, otherwise it will not to avoid breaking the game
- Increased parallel track separation and track clearance to more realistic values
- Cost multipliers are now based off of shape based area instead of a linear relationship
    - Aka bores are calculated as circles now
    - Also taking into account placement of track in the tube
- New tunnel costs are based off of said new track separation values
- For the sake of avoiding the bricking of prior saves, the new track geometry and associcated numbers are not retroactively applied.
    - I have not tested this but honestly I would be shocked if changing track parallel spacing for already laid track *didn't* brick a save
- Track multiplier explanations have been removed temporarily until a new UI is ready for them
    - The multipliers themselves are still there which is more important. Just know wider and/or catenary = tunnels more expensive. 
- Fixed the MBTA Blue Line behaving as if the entire line is *both* catenary and third rail
    - Now it uses catenary above ground and third-rail below

danield1909/dantrains.name: Version 1.1.2 "Phoenix" (Hotfix)
danield1909/dantrains.body:
- Fixed a glitch prevent legacy trains from being imported correctly

Thank you to Noahhhhhhhh from the SubwayBuilder Discord for help fixing this one

danield1909/dantrains.name: Version 1.1.1 "Phoenix" (Hotfix)
danield1909/dantrains.body: - Actually fixed the glitch causing crashes on menu start for new users

danield1909/dantrains.name: Version 1.1.0 "Phoenix" (Minor Update)
danield1909/dantrains.body:
- Hopefully fixed a glitch causing crashes on menu start
- Added Singapore (MRT and LRT)
- Added more tags (NYC A Division, NYC B Division, Future Tech, and Ansaldo-Breda Driverless Metros)
- Added unit conversions for those who are uncivilized
- Added depth multipliers (I am sorry for taking this long to add them)
- Expanded NYC rolling stock to include the entire current fleet + the future planned ones
- Added a new type of metro called the Weird Metro. This is for the trains which are metros but still have level crossings for some bizzare reason
- Made the description text smaller

danield1909/dantrains.name: Version 1.0.3 "Phoenix" (Mini Patch)
danield1909/dantrains.body:
- Added support for light mode
- Fixed some glitches with the main-menu individual train registration
- Added an in game notification when trains get registered (does not work for main menu)
- Added a disable of the register button after it's already been pressed to prevent accidental spam
- Sorted trains alphabetically instead of by length

- Fixed a glitch which led to trains being deleted from a train save file upon re-save
- Added second menu to help recover said deleted trains
- Fixed a duplication error

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/DanielD1909/danTrains/compare/v1.0.1...v1.0.2

danield1909/dantrains.name: Version 1.0.0 "Phoenix"
danield1909/dantrains.body:
Welcome to the release of danTrains 1.0.0 "Phoenix"!

There's been a lot of changes since the last version so here's a broad summary changelog:
- danTrains is now a standalone mod with no other requirements. This was done primarily due to the outdatedness of addTrains.
- Three new menus have been added, two as pop ups in game and one on the main menu. The instructions will explain how to use them.
    - Panel 1 - Train Registration: Allows you to register trains in game
    - Panel 2 - Train Dictionary: Allows you to search through all the train types in more detail in game,
    - Panel 3 - Main Menu Save Loader: Allows saves to not break
- Perth, Australia has been added with 3 trains thanks to Pio from the SubwayBuilder discord
- Tags have been directly made visible
- danTrains has been added to Railyard (unless you were like super quick to this in which case I may be doing it rn)
- Mod structure has been changed to allow easier updating of train data which should provide faster train updates in the future
- Automation, Electrification, Track Gauge, and Loading Gauge are all things you can now choose for all trains when adding them
    - In the future this will affect track compatibility but the game cannot handle that yet
- Train speed on curves has been adjusted to match real life standards (based on track geometry but converted to lateral acceleration bc that is what the game uses)
- All costs have been set to approximate Mediterranean prices
    - I was not able to find good sources on my own so I will admit I asked ChatGPT for Italian prices and then did some scrutinizing
    - If you have better sources let me know
    - Train cost per hour is a very American metric as in Europe they do cost per km per passenger or just cost per km
    - This has basically universally lowered prices which does make the game easier but it is what everyone voted for
    - Commuter Rail is still very expensive tho. Because it is in real life.
- Spreadsheet has not been updated and is no longer a priority due to the existence of the in-game dictionary
- Lateral acceleration limits no longer affected by automation. 
- Added 1.2.1 beta compatibility for the new station length feature.
- Once registered, descriptions now include track type and creator. Trying to figure out the best way to do tags.
- Continents have been replaced with Regions since continents are big and hard to define while regions are smaller and slightly easier to define
- Train Dropdown is now typable inside of so you can just start typing and find what you want.
- Various other minor things I cannot remember at this exact moment 

PLEASE NOTE: NEW FEATURES ARE A LITTLE FINNICKY BUT NOTHING SHOULD BE GAMEBREAKING. I OFFER NO GUARANTEES THOUGH, JUST MY EXPERIENCE.

INSTRUCTIONS:

1. Registering trains:
    1. To register a train you need to open the registration tab once you are in a game and ensure Automation, Electrification, Track Gauge, and Loading Gauge are all set
    2. Click Preview
    3. Review the stats and see the description and also make sure your train lengths are right
    4. Click register to register
    5. Check to make sure it worked
    6. Save the game with a specific name so you can use it in the future (THINGS DO NOT SAVE WITHOUT AT LEAST AN AUTOSAVE, AND AN AUTOSAVE WILL OVERWRITE ANY OTHER AUTOSAVES)
2. Loading Saves:
    1. Locate the big panel in the main menu that should have "Dan Trains" in it
    2. Navigate the dropdown to the save you want to play.
    3. Press the white button
    4. Load your save as normal
3. Managing Save Data:
    1. If you want to delete any data, go to the panel at the front.
    2. Select the save data you want to delete from the dropdown.
    3. Press the red button.
    4. As a failsafe, you need to press a second (formerly dark red, now red) button
    5. If you click on the dropdown you should see that it is gone.
4. IMPORTING LEGACY SAVES:
    1. First, make sure the trains are DanTrains Gen 1 Trains and not from some other mod. I cannot import those ones.
    2. Open SubwayBuilder with the following mods enabled:
        1. [DanTrains Gen1 (0.3.1) (NEW VERSION)](https://github.com/DanielD1909/danTrainsGen1/releases/tag/Final)
        2. addTrains DT (comes with 0.3.1 DanTrains)
        3. DanTrains 1.0.0 Phoenix
    3. Open the game with the correct trains enabled via addTrains.
    4. Save the game (probably with a new name just in case)
    5. Exit to menu and then disable danTrains 0.3.1 and addTrains DT
    6. Open the game
    7. Follow Loading Saves instructions

gio1112/subway-builder-citymapper.name: v0.2.2 | Performance Update!
gio1112/subway-builder-citymapper.body:
- Debounced build-action resync triggers so duplicate hooks only schedule one resync pass per action.
- Moved OSM v6 parsing to a Web Worker path with automatic fallback to main-thread parsing if worker processing fails.
- Optimized station coverage checks using grid-bucket spatial partitioning instead of full cross-product distance scans.
- Reduced unnecessary map layer reorder work by checking current layer order before issuing moveLayer calls.
- Reduced style-driven reapply pressure by only queueing overlay resync when core overlay layers are actually missing.
- Added lifecycle cleanup for worker and timers on game-end reset to avoid stale background work.

gio1112/subway-builder-citymapper.name: v0.2.1
gio1112/subway-builder-citymapper.body: Added a lighter panel theme for the game's light mode.

gio1112/subway-builder-citymapper.name: V0.2.0 | Major | Semi-Stable
gio1112/subway-builder-citymapper.body:
Major update - redesigned overlay panel with layer toggles, persistent IndexedDB cache restored, faster and more reliable layer refresh after build/style changes, improved platform and station mode detection (including London Underground tagging edge cases because they are tagged funny), and stronger top-layer rendering behavior.

Haven't tested on all maps, only maps tested: NYC + LDN, happy for testers.

gio1112/subway-builder-citymapper.name: v0.1.2 | Minor efficiency changes
gio1112/subway-builder-citymapper.body: Made the code more efficient, thats really all, should be better and fix some bugs maybe fps too.

stefanorigano/advanced_analytics.name: v1.4.0
stefanorigano/advanced_analytics.body:
🆕 Timetable Analysis
Four new real-time metrics in the Route panel:

- Headway Regularity: detects train bunching
- Schedule Drift: shows whether your route is running ahead or behind the timetable
- Delay Profile: per-stop breakdown of average arrival delays, colour-coded by severity
- Dwell Compliance: reveals which stations are holding trains longer than scheduled

🆕  Route notes: write custom notes in the route panel

📊 Route panel redesign: cleaner layout, better information hierarchy. Added "All-time profit"

⚡ Load Factor now reacts instantly to train schedule changes. Adding or removing trains updates the reading immediately without waiting for the next data cycle

🌙 Smoother midnight transition. Metrics no longer spike or reset abruptly when the in-game day rolls over

📖 Guide updated with full explanations for all four timetable metrics

[IMP] smooth midnight transition

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/stefanorigano/advanced_analytics/compare/v1.3.1...v1.3.2

stefanorigano/advanced_analytics.name: v1.3.1
stefanorigano/advanced_analytics.body:
Recalibrate load-factor thresholds to reflect real world scenario.
A route averaging 50% across the day likely hits 80–90% at rush hour,
which is healthy service, not "underused".
The healthy green band is now 40–80%, and only routes below 20% are
flagged red as under-served.

New thresholds:
- CRITICAL_LOW 40→20,
- WARNING_LOW 55→40,
- CRITICAL_HIGH 90→95.

stefanorigano/advanced_analytics.name: v1.3.0
stefanorigano/advanced_analytics.body:
New Features & Metric Refinement
- Load Factor Metric: Introduced a new analytic metric to track passenger density and vehicle capacity utilization across your subway lines.
- Hourly Data Aggregation: All major metrics (including Load Factor) are now tracked on an hourly basis, allowing you to identify peak congestion times and troughs throughout the day-night cycle.
- Performance Metric (formerly "Usage"): Transformed into a dimensionless multiplier measuring how much ridership a route generates relative to its bidirectional throughput capacity.

Check the "User Guide" for all the details

UI & Visual Enhancements
- System Statistics Dashboard: Refined the GaugeArc design for a cleaner, more readable visual representation of network health.
- Navigation: Updated the PanelBreadcrumb design to improve menu navigation and layout consistency.
- Tooltips: Overhauled tooltip designs

Technical Improvements & Bug Fixes
- LifeCycle Management: Fixed a logic error in lifecycle timing by implementing strict greater-than (>) comparisons.
- Optimized data processing by fully adopting the Accumulator Pattern, eliminating redundant logic and improving performance during high-traffic periods.
- Code Cleanup: Removed deprecated files and legacy logic that were superseded by the new accumulator architecture.

To install the mod manually:
1. Crate the advanced-analytics folder in into your mods directory ( Main Menu > Settings > Mods)
2.  Extract the ZIP content into the advanced-analytics folder you created.
3. Restart the game and activate the "Advanced Analytics"
